entries.definition: Stereoscopic format that places the left and right eye images next to each other in a single frame. Many glasses-free displays expect SBS or similar layouts.
How strongly objects appear to pop out of or sink into the screen. Higher depth increases the 3D effect but can cause discomfort if pushed too far.
The point where left and right views align. Adjusting convergence shifts where depth feels anchored in the scene.
Distance between the virtual left and right cameras. Works with depth and convergence to control overall stereoscopic intensity.
Native — built-in 3D support. Optimized — verified preset. Playable — community-tested. Experimental — may need tweaks. Unsupported — not recommended.
Hardware class derived from detected VRAM: Low, Medium, High, or Enthusiast. Guides render scale and post-processing defaults.
The toolchain used to start a title in 3D — for example UEVR, ReShade, TrueGame, or a display vendor hub. Shown in the launch preview before Play in 3D.
Launches with minimal injection so you can verify the game starts before applying full 3D presets. Rollback restores prior configs if launch fails.
Laptops with both integrated and discrete GPUs. Ensure the discrete GPU is active for 3D titles to avoid low performance or black screens.
